title: Player Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Accessibility, and Evidence · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs player outcomes, repository baseline, accessibility, and evidence from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.
Translate fun, fairness, responsiveness, and clarity into repeatable playtest tasks, traces, thresholds, and changed cases.
Inspect status, diff, revision, ignore rules, environment files, assets, licenses, and existing tests before editing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Player Outcome · changed case · Review Player Outcome
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 1594 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Define player, play goal, core verbs, feedback, consequence, session length, and observable success before choosing mechanics.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 4500 during a controller-remapping accessibility review. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then lose window focus while thrust and fire are held, then prove both actions release safely. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: A passing unit test proves the game feels responsive and accessible.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence. Artifact under review: player, repository, accessibility, and evidence charter.

options.text: The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Evidence Ladder, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 6119 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Evidence Ladder precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A review Asteroids team handles case 6119 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the game charter on player value, repository state, accessibility, and observable playtest evidence; keep every critical cue available through more than color or sound and every action remappable without timing precision; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate pure simulation, headless integration, visual review, device playtest, accessibility review, package, and release evidence.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Evidence Ladder, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: A passing unit test proves the game feels responsive and accessible.
